classification.reasoning: All hunks in the patch were found in the target branch with exact matches or very high confidence fuzzy matches, indicating the patch applies cleanly without structural or API changes. The changes are additive and straightforward, mostly adding new methods, constants, and tests without modifying existing logic or structure. Therefore, this patch is a trivial backport classified as TYPE_I.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Adjusted formatting by adding a blank line after a method return statement.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: org.apache.hadoop.conf.Configuration, unnamed method returning trimmed string collection
hunk_descriptions.intent: Improve code readability and maintain consistent formatting.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of HashMap to support new map-related utility methods.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Enable usage of HashMap in newly added methods handling key-value string parsing.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Introduced a new static method that parses a string containing comma or newline separated key=value pairs into a Map<String, String>.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Provide utility to convert configuration strings with key=value pairs into a map for easier access and processing.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a new static method to split a string by '=' character with trimming, returning an array of strings.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Support parsing of key=value pairs by splitting on '=' with whitespace trimming, used by the new map parsing method.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a new constant string key for AWS credentials provider mapping configuration.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Introduce a configuration key to specify mappings of AWS credentials providers using key/value pairs.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of HashMap to support new map-related utility methods.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Enable usage of HashMap in utility methods for parsing configuration strings into maps.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a new static method that retrieves a configuration property by name and parses its value as a map of key=value pairs using comma and newline delimiters.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Provide a utility to extract and parse configuration properties into maps for easier credential provider mapping handling.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of the new AWS_CREDENTIALS_PROVIDER_MAPPING constant.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Enable usage of the new configuration key for AWS credentials provider mappings in the credential provider factory.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added retrieval of AWS credentials provider mappings from configuration as a map of key-value pairs.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: org.apache.hadoop.fs.s3a.auth.CredentialProviderListFactory, inside method initializing credential provider mappings
hunk_descriptions.intent: Support custom mapping of AWS credentials provider class names from configuration to enable flexible provider resolution.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Enhanced credential provider class name resolution logic to check for mappings from the new AWS credentials provider mapping configuration and apply mapped class names accordingly.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: org.apache.hadoop.fs.s3a.auth.CredentialProviderListFactory, inside method processing credential provider class names
hunk_descriptions.intent: Allow remapping of credential provider class names based on user-defined configuration to support compatibility or customization.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of HashMap to support new map-related utility methods.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Enable use of HashMap for new methods handling key-value string parsing.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Introduced a new static method that parses a string containing comma or newline separated key=value pairs into a Map, trimming whitespace appropriately.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Provide utility to convert configuration strings with key=value pairs into a Map for easier access and manipulation.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a new static method to split a string by '=' character with trimming, returning an array of strings.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Support parsing of key=value strings by splitting on '=' with whitespace trimming for utility methods.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a new constant string key for AWS credentials provider mapping configuration.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Introduce a configuration key to support mapping of AWS credentials providers via key/value pairs.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of HashMap to support new map-related utility methods.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Enable use of HashMap for new methods handling key-value string parsing.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a new static method that retrieves a configuration property by name and parses its value as a map of key=value pairs, using the utility method from StringUtils.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Provide a convenient way to obtain configuration properties as key-value maps for easier credential provider mapping and other uses.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of the new AWS_CREDENTIALS_PROVIDER_MAPPING constant to support credential provider mapping logic.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Enable use of the new configuration key for AWS credentials provider mapping in credential provider factory.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added retrieval of AWS credentials provider mappings from configuration as a map of key-value pairs, using the new utility method.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: org.apache.hadoop.fs.s3a.auth.CredentialProviderListFactory, inside method initializing credential provider mappings
hunk_descriptions.intent: Support dynamic remapping of AWS credentials provider class names based on configuration to allow flexible credential provider substitution.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Extended the credential provider remapping logic to check the new AWS credentials provider mapping configuration and apply remapping if a mapping exists.
hunk_descriptions.intent: Allow credentials provider class names to be remapped based on user-defined mappings, enhancing flexibility and compatibility with different AWS SDK versions or custom providers.
